figures.caption: Table 11: ACC×BLEU scores (%) on WikiMatrix for Gemma 3 1B, Gemma 3 4B, Llama 3.1 8B. Best result per language within each model is bolded.
Table 12: ACC×BLEU scores on Tatoeba for Gemma 3 1B, Gemma 3 4B, Llama 3.1 8B. Best result per language within each model is bolded.
Table 3: Hyperparameter search space. Tuning is conducted on Chinese, Spanish, French, and German for every dataset and model combination.
Figures 13–15 and Table 8 show the ablation results for Gemma 3 4B. Results are generally consistent with Llama 3.1 8B.

Figure 1: Overview of LangFIR. (1) For each target-language sentence, a random-token sequence is generated by sampling from the tokenizer’s vocabulary. (2) Residual-stream activations are extracted at a layer l and encoded through SAE. (3) Sample-wise filtering is performed with activation frequency threshold τ to identify features that activate frequently for target-language sentences (language-consistent) and for random-token sequences (languageagnostic). (4) Removing language-agnostic features from language-consistent features yields a sparse set of language-specific features.

Figure 4: Change in cross-entropy after ablating the top-2 language-specific features per layer on Llama 3.1 8B. Ablation increases loss selectively for the target language. English is a notable exception.

Figure 15: Ablation of frequency threshold τ and length ratio between random-token sequences and target-language sentences on Gemma 3 4B. (a) Performance generally trends upward with increasing τ and stabilizes around τ = 0.8. (b) Steering metrics remain generally stable across length ratios, showing that exact length matching is not required.

Figure 8: Layer-wise analyses on Llama 3.1 8B. (a) Feature density dips in middle layers and recovers in later layers. (b) Activation magnitudes peak in later layers, indicating stronger language specialization near the output.

Figure 18: Ablation of frequency threshold τ and length ratio between random-token sequences and target-language sentences on Gemma 3 1B. (a) Performance trends upward with increasing τ and stabilizes around τ = 0.8. (b) Steering metrics remain stable across length ratios, showing that exact length matching is not required.

Figure 17: Ablation of intervention layer and scaling coefficient α on Gemma 3 1B. (a) ACC×BLEU peaks in middle layers, where steering redirects language without disrupting content. (b) Intermediate α balances steering strength and translation quality.
Table 1: ACC×BLEU averaged over FLORES+, WikiMatrix, and Tatoeba for Gemma 3 1B, Gemma 3 4B, Llama 3.1 8B. Best result per language within each model is bolded.

Figure 10: Change in cross-entropy after ablating the top-2 language-specific features per layer on Llama 3.1 8B. Consistent with Figure 4, ablation selectively increases loss for the target language.
Table 6: Selected hyperparameters for Llama-3.1-8B.
Table 10: ACC×BLEU scores on FLORES+ for Gemma 3 1B, Gemma 3 4B, Llama 3.1 8B. Best result per language within each model is bolded.

Figure 5: Ablation of number of top-k language-specific features, sample size, and featureoverlap removal on Llama 3.1 8B. (a) Even k=1 achieves strong performance. (b) Steering metrics are stable with as few as 10 sentences. (c) Removing feature-overlap removal degrades all steering metrics dramatically.

Figure 12: Ablation of frequency threshold τ and length ratio between random-token sequences and target-language sentences on Llama 3.1 8B. (a) Performance trends upward with increasing τ and stabilizes around τ = 0.8. (b) Steering metrics remain stable across length ratios, showing that exact length matching is not required.

Figure 11: Ablation of intervention layer and scaling coefficient α on Llama 3.1 8B. (a) ACC×BLEU peaks in middle layers, where steering redirects language without disrupting content. (b) Intermediate α balances steering strength and translation quality.

Figure 2: Analyses of language-specific feature identification across sample sizes on Llama 3.1 8B. (a) Overlap between language-consistent and language-agnostic feature sets approaches 100%. (b) Fewer than 5 language-specific features remain per language. Both quantities stabilize by ∼100 sample size.
Table 4: Selected hyperparameters for Gemma-3-1B.
Table 5: Selected hyperparameters for Gemma-3-4B.
